supported_future_modes.description: Project generated public data summaries into old-client-friendly read views.
Serve a verified static snapshot tree to local or LAN clients after snapshot consumer policy exists.
Project plain text summaries for text browsers, terminals, and screen readers.
Expose manifests, checksums, and file-tree indexes as read-only public data.
Let a modern trusted host mediate optional future live backend results without exposing unsafe old-client control paths.

future_protocol_candidates.label: Local static HTTP projection
future_protocol_candidates.notes: Would require an operator-controlled bind policy before any listener exists.

future_protocol_candidates.label: Local text HTTP projection
future_protocol_candidates.notes: Would expose plain text projections only after relay runtime policy exists.

future_protocol_candidates.label: Local file-tree HTTP projection
future_protocol_candidates.notes: Would serve read-only manifests and checksums only.

future_protocol_candidates.label: Read-only FTP-style mirror
future_protocol_candidates.notes: Insecure transport would be public/read-only only and would not carry credentials.

future_protocol_candidates.label: Read-only SMB projection
future_protocol_candidates.notes: Requires platform and permissions review before implementation.

future_protocol_candidates.label: Read-only NFS projection
future_protocol_candidates.notes: Requires export-root and LAN trust review before implementation.

future_protocol_candidates.label: Experimental Gopher-style projection
future_protocol_candidates.notes: Experimental old-client candidate only; not implemented.

future_protocol_candidates.label: Native client sidecar
future_protocol_candidates.notes: Would require native-client contract, private-data policy, and explicit operator controls.

future_protocol_candidates.label: Static snapshot mount or browse root
future_protocol_candidates.notes: Depends on future snapshot consumer and verification policy.

trust_and_integrity_model.resolver_truth_source: governed source/evidence records and static snapshot manifests
trust_and_integrity_model.insecure_transport_limit: Insecure future transports may carry only public read-only data and cannot provide authenticity by themselves.

operator_controls_required: verify no write/admin routes for old clients
verify live probes remain disabled unless future policy explicitly enables them
security_requirements: local or trusted LAN by default
no admin routes to old clients
no write actions to old clients
future threat model required before implementation
privacy_requirements: no private user state by default
no private local paths by default
no account or session data over insecure transports
logs must avoid private queries or paths unless a future policy explicitly permits them
local cache exposure requires separate operator signoff
next_milestones: Rust Source Registry Parity Catch-up v0
Rust Local Index Parity Planning v0
Native Action / Download / Install Policy v0
notes: Relay Surface Design v0 is contract, inventory, and checklist work only.
No relay runtime, socket listener, or protocol server is introduced.
The relay remains future/deferred in surface capability and route matrices.
Snapshots, lite, text, files, and public data are the current compatibility path.
Native Client Contract v0 is required before any native sidecar or native relay control surface.
Manual Observation Batch 0 remains human-operated parallel work.
Internet Archive live probe work remains gated by explicit human approval and live probe gateway review.
